overview: The TS Filter processes **Qt TS (Translation Source)** resource files, implementing the `IFilter` interface. It is based on the [TS specification](http://doc.trolltech.com/4.3/linguist-ts-file-format.html) from Qt/Trolltech. The filter handles extraction of translatable content from `<translation>` elements, mapping the `type` attribute to approval states, and supports inline code detection via configurable regular expressions.

output.escapeGT.help: Controls whether the `>` character is escaped as `>` in the output TS file. By default, XML only requires escaping `<` and `&`, but some tools or workflows may expect `>` to also be escaped.

output.decodeByteValues.help:
Converts `<byte value="[value]"/>` elements in the TS document to their raw character equivalents.

TS files use `<byte/>` elements to represent characters that are invalid in XML. If left disabled, these elements are preserved as **inline codes**.

**Recommendation:** Keep disabled if your downstream workflow uses XML-based formats like XLIFF or TMX, since the raw characters may be invalid in those formats as well.
notes: Characters stored as `<byte/>` elements are invalid in XML â€” converting them to raw characters may cause issues in XML-based interchange formats like XLIFF or TMX.

inlineCodes.help:
Defines regular expressions to detect inline codes within extracted text. Any match is converted to an inline code placeholder.

The default expression captures:
- **printf-style format specifiers**: `%d`, `%s`, `%2$s`, `%-10.2f`, etc.
- **Escape sequences**: `\n`, `\r\n`, `\t`, `\a`, `\b`, `\f`, `\v`
- **Numbered placeholders**: `{0}`, `{1 something}`, etc.

processingNotes: Line-break style in the output matches the original input file (no normalization).

The `<translation>` element's `type` attribute controls extraction behavior: no attribute â†’ approved (`yes`), `unfinished` â†’ not approved (`no`), `obsolete` â†’ **not extracted** (treated as unused entries).
The `<translatorcomment>` element is stored as a `Property.TRANSNOTE` on the text unit, accessible by subsequent pipeline steps or editors.
limitations: There are sometimes issues with extracting and merging back `<numerusform>` elements. Perform a **round-trip test** before sending for translation.

examples.title: Default inline code pattern
examples.description: The default regex pattern used for detecting inline codes in TS content. It matches printf-style placeholders, escape sequences, and numbered placeholders.

examples.title: Translation type mapping
examples.description: Shows how the `type` attribute on `<translation>` elements maps to extraction behavior.
examples.output:
```text
First message: extracted, target approved = yes
Second message: extracted, target approved = no
Third message: NOT extracted (obsolete)
```
